title: ceiba aesculifolia subsp. aesculifolia
intro: Ceiba aesculifolia subsp. aesculifolia, also known as the kapok tree, is a species of Ceiba, a genus of flowering plants in the family Malvaceae. It is a large tree native to Central and South America, known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Ceiba aesculifolia subsp. aesculifolia include reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Ceiba aesculifolia subsp. aesculifolia is traditionally used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and topical creams, to leverage its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
